ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-03-2023 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-12-2023 Continued To May-16-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Niloufar Mazhari, Esq. appearing for the plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to May 16, 2023 at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: S. Le; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In
The Alternative, Evidenciary Sanctions; Request For
Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LAURO MARTIN's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Alternative, Evidentiary Sanctions; Request For Monetary Sanctions: is granted in part and denied in part.

Defendant Martin's motion for terminating sanctions since, at this stage of litigation, terminating sanctions is premature/inappropriate and is not supported by the record. "Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.450, subdivision (d) authorizes a trial court to impose an issue, evidence, or terminating sanction under Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.030 if a party or party-affiliated deponent fails to obey an order compelling attendance, testimony, and production. Section 2023.030 authorizes a trial court to impose monetary sanctions, issue sanctions, evidence sanctions, or terminating sanctions against anyone engaging in conduct that is a misuse of the discovery process. Subdivision (d) of section 2023.030 provides, The court may impose a terminating sanction by one of the following orders: (1) An order striking out the pleadings or parts of the pleadings of any party engaging in the misuse of the discovery process. (2) An order staying further proceedings by that party until an order for discovery is obeyed. (3) An order dismissing the action, or any part of the action, of that party. (4) An order rendering a judgment by default against that party. The discovery statutes evince an incremental approach to discovery sanctions, starting with monetary sanctions and ending with the ultimate sanction of termination. Discovery sanctions "should be appropriate to the dereliction, and should not exceed that which is required to protect the interests of the party entitled to but denied discovery. If a lesser sanction fails to curb misuse, a greater sanction is warranted: continuing misuses of the discovery process warrant incrementally harsher sanctions until the sanction is reached that will curb the abuse. A decision to order terminating sanctions should not be made lightly. But where a violation is willful, preceded by a history of abuse, and the evidence shows that less severe sanctions would not produce compliance with the discovery rules, the trial court is justified in imposing the ultimate sanction. The trial court may order a terminating sanction for discovery abuse after considering the totality of the circumstances: the conduct of the party to determine if the actions were willful; the detriment to the propounding party; and the number of formal and informal attempts to obtain the discovery." Creed-21 v. City of Wildomar, 18 Cal. App. 5th 690, 701-702 (2017). (Internal quotations and citations omitted.) See also Aghaian v. Minassian, 64 Cal. App. 5th 603, 618-619 (2021).

Additionally, Defendant Martin's alternative motion for issue/evidentiary sanctions is denied without prejudice for Defendant Martin's failure to provide Separate Statement as required by California Rules of Court, rule 3.1345(a)(7). CRC 3.1345(a)(7); Mills v. U.S. Bank, 166 Cal. App. 4th 871, 892-893 (2008).

Defendant Martin's motion for monetary sanctions is granted as to Plaintiff Scott Litwak and his counsel S. Sean Bral, Esq., jointly and severally, in the amount of $2,310.00 as the Court finds that Plaintiff Scott Litwak has acted without substantial justification. The payment of sanctions shall be made to Defendant's counsel on or before December 31, 2022. Plaintiff Scott Litwak is further ordered to attend his deposition on or before December 31, 2022 at a time/place that is convenient for both parties.

Prevailing party to send proposed order tracking the adopted ruling of the court via email to williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Continued To Nov-01-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned $400.00 for failure to appear. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to November 1, 2022 at 10:30am in Department 610. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; Clerk: GGonzales; Proceedings not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Shirin R. Delkhah In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. HARDIP PASSANANTI, Esq. specially appearing for attorney S. Sean Bral, Esq. of Bral & Associates (Tel: 310-789-2007) for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this case.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LAURO MARTIN's Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for plaintiff(both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

Plaintiff's counsel requests to extend the deadline for time to respond. However, the Court notes that plaintiff's counsel did not notify the other side of her intent to appear to this morning. As such, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion is granted, no opposition filed. Plaintiff Scott Litwak shall appear for deposition with records and testify on a mutually convenient date within 45 days. Plaintiff Scott Litwak shall pay sanctions in the amount of $2,020 to defendant's attorney Toschi Collins Doyle within 30 days.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
